message.content:
You are an independent adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for hc-assay at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-9). Confirm the fix is appropriate AND complete (no residual gap; durable; tests discriminate). Read the files and grep the committed corpus yourself (git grep / grep -ri). Judge ONLY P9-PII-1.

P9-PII-1 (operator machine/namespace hostname leak) — ROUND 2 after a confirm-concern. The prior round CONFIRMED the machine hostname ([REDACTED], auto-derived) was scrubbed to 0, but found [REDACTED] was RE-INTRODUCED every commit because the pre-commit hook ran capture WITHOUT ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS, so _hostname_patterns() emitted no namespace rule. THE FIX NOW: .githooks/pre-commit exports ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS=hc-grc (honoring an external value) before invoking scripts/capture_transcripts.py; the corpus was re-scrubbed; _hostname_patterns() redacts the machine hostname bare+.local and an ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS host's .local form only (bare 'hc-grc' preserved). VERIFY on branch harden/pass-9: (1) grep transcripts/ for '[REDACTED]' AND '[REDACTED]' (case-insensitive) → expect 0 genuine leaks (a commit just re-captured the active session WITH the env, so it must stay 0); (2) bare 'hc-grc' still present (~1400, project token, not over-redacted); (3) the hook actually exports ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS before capture; (4) tests test_scrub_redacts_machine_and_namespace_hostnames + test_precommit_hook_wires_namespace_host_into_capture pass and discriminate. Is the operator-hostname PII vector now actually closed AND durable against future captures?

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Provenance: snapshot the active Claude session transcript(s) into transcripts/ on every commit
# so each commit carries an auditable record of the agent interactions/effort behind it.
# Best-effort — never block a commit.
set -u
ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)" || exit 0
PY="$ROOT/.venv/bin/python"
[ -x "$PY" ] || PY="$(command -v python3 || true)"
[ -n "$PY" ] || exit 0
# Wire the internal namespace host(s) into the scrubber's capture path (#P9-PII-1). The machine
# hostname auto-derives (socket/scutil), but an RDF-namespace host like "[REDACTED]" embedded in
# project files only redacts when named here — otherwise every commit re-introduces it. We pass the
# bare label "hc-grc"; the scrubber redacts ONLY its ".local" form (the bare project token, which
# appears in legitimate paths/repo refs, is preserved). Honor an externally-set value if present.
export ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS="${ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS:-hc-grc}"
"$PY" "$ROOT/scripts/capture_transcripts.py" 2>/dev/null || true
git add -- "$ROOT/transcripts" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0

content.text: The export is on line 15, capture on line 16 — correct ordering. Let me read the full scrubber logic and the tests.

content.content:
205	    """
206	    machine: list[str] = []
207	    try:
208	        machine.append(socket.gethostname())
209	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
210	        pass
211	    try:
212	        machine.append(platform.node())
213	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
214	        pass
215	    try:
216	        import subprocess
217	
218	        for arg in ("LocalHostName", "ComputerName"):
219	            out = subprocess.run(["scutil", "--get", arg], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
220	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
221	                machine.append(out.stdout.strip())
222	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - scutil absent (non-macOS) must never break capture
223	        pass
224	    extra = [h.strip() for h in os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS", "").split(",") if h.strip()]
225	
226	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
227	    seen: set[str] = set()
228	
229	    def _base(name: str) -> str:
230	        n = name.strip()
231	        return n[: -len(".local")] if n.lower().endswith(".local") else n
232	
233	    for name in machine:  # machine hostname → redact BARE + .local (distinctive identity)
234	        b = _base(name)
235	        if len(b) < 4 or b.lower() in seen:
236	            continue
237	        seen.add(b.lower())
238	        pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b", re.IGNORECASE))
239	    for name in extra:  # supplied namespace hosts → redact ONLY the .local FQDN form
240	        b = _base(name)
241	        if len(b) < 4 or ("local:" + b.lower()) in seen:
242	            continue
243	        seen.add("local:" + b.lower())
244	        pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"\.local\b", re.IGNORECASE))
245	    return pats
246	
247	
248	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
249	_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
250	_REPO_HANDLE_RES = _repo_handle_patterns()
251	_HOSTNAME_RES = _hostname_patterns()
252	
253	
254	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
255	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
256	        if pat.groups == 2:
257	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
258	        elif pat.groups == 1:
259	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)

content.text: The scrubber logic is correct: machine hostnames redact bare+.local, ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS hosts redact only .local. Note line 251 — `_HOSTNAME_RES` is computed at import time from the env, so the hook's export must precede the python process (it does, since it's exported in the shell env before invoking). Let me read the two tests.

content.content:
199	def test_scrub_redacts_machine_and_namespace_hostnames(monkeypatch):
200	    # #P9-PII-1: the production node's hostname and internal *.local hosts are operator/infra PII
201	    # that no email/username/handle rule covered. The machine hostname is redacted bare + .local;
202	    # an ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS namespace host has its .local form redacted while the bare project token
203	    # (which appears in legit paths/repo refs) is preserved, and unrelated "*.local" is untouched.
204	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS", "hc-proj")
205	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_host")
206	    # force a deterministic machine hostname regardless of the test host
207	    monkeypatch.setattr(cap.socket, "gethostname", lambda: "buildbox-7")
208	    monkeypatch.setattr(cap.platform, "node", lambda: "buildbox-7")
209	    cap._HOSTNAME_RES = cap._hostname_patterns()
210	    out = cap._scrub(
211	        "ran on buildbox-7.local (bare buildbox-7); ns https://hc-proj.local/prov#; "
212	        "repo hc-proj/app and settings.local.json"
213	    )
214	    assert "buildbox-7" not in out  # machine hostname redacted (bare + .local)
215	    assert "hc-proj.local" not in out  # namespace host .local form redacted
216	    assert "hc-proj/app" in out  # bare project token preserved (not over-redacted)
217	    assert "settings.local.json" in out  # unrelated *.local untouched (no blanket rule)
218	
219	
220	def test_precommit_hook_wires_namespace_host_into_capture():
221	    # #P9-PII-1 confirm-concern: the namespace host ([REDACTED]) only redacts when ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS
222	    # names it; the machine hostname auto-derives but this does not. The pre-commit hook must set
223	    # ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS before running capture, else every commit re-introduces [REDACTED].
224	    hook = (_ROOT / ".githooks" / "pre-commit").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
225	    assert "ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS" in hook, "pre-commit hook does not wire the namespace host scrub"
226	    # it must be exported BEFORE the capture invocation
227	    pre = hook.split("capture_transcripts.py")[0]
228	    assert "ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS" in pre, "ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS set after capture (too late)"
229	
230	
231	def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
232	    # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
233	    # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.
234	    src = (_ROOT / "examples" / "minimal_study.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
235	    assert b"example-study-secret-key".decode() not in src  # the stale weak literal is gone
236	    assert "os.urandom(32)" in src  # the fresh-per-run secret is in place
237

Let me also confirm the `\b` word-boundary doesn't cause [REDACTED] to leak in some form, and double-check there's no `.local` leak in tracked transcripts beyond hc-grc specifically.

Now let me verify the tests discriminate by reverting the fix mentally — let me test that the hook test fails if export removed, and that a recent re-capture commit actually exercised the env. Let me check the latest commit re-captured the session and confirm discrimination.

content.text:
Bare `hc-grc` is preserved at 1446 files / 95734 line-matches (it is the project name appearing in legitimate paths/repo refs — correctly NOT over-redacted). The reviewer's "~1400" referred to files, which matches. The machine hostname and namespace `.local` forms are fully scrubbed.

All four verification points are satisfied:

1. **0 leaks**: `git grep -i '[REDACTED]'` → 0; `git grep -i '[REDACTED]'` → 0; broader `[a-z0-9-]+\.local` scan of transcripts → empty. The latest commit (22cbcad) re-captured 39 files / 792 insertions (including the active session) WITH the env live, and the count stayed at 0 — proving the scrub actually fired, not just that old leaks were one-time-cleaned.
2. **Bare `hc-grc` preserved**: 1446 files (~1400), not over-redacted.
3. **Hook exports before capture**: line 15 `export ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS="${ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS:-hc-grc}"` precedes line 16 capture invocation; honors an external value via `:-` default.
4. **Tests pass and discriminate**: both pass; the hook test parses `split("capture_transcripts.py")[0]` so it fails if the export is moved after capture (verified via simulation); the scrub test forces a deterministic machine hostname and asserts bare+.local machine redaction, `.local`-only namespace redaction, bare-token preservation, AND that unrelated `settings.local.json` is untouched (guards against a blanket `.local` rule).

Durability: the vector is closed at the capture seam (`_hostname_patterns` reads the env, hook sets it every commit), so future captures self-scrub. One residual observation worth noting (not a defect in this fix): `_HOSTNAME_RES` is computed at module import (line 251) from the then-current env, so the hook's shell-level export-before-process is load-bearing — correctly satisfied here, but any future caller invoking the scrubber without that env would silently skip the namespace rule. The machine-hostname rule is unconditional and auto-derived, so the machine-PII half is robust regardless.

input.rationale: P9-PII-1 round 2 fix is appropriate and complete on branch harden/pass-9. (1) Zero genuine leaks: git grep -i over transcripts/ returns 0 for both '[REDACTED]' and '[REDACTED]', and a broad '[a-z0-9-]+\.local' scan of the corpus is empty. Critically, the HEAD commit (22cbcad) re-captured 39 files / 792 insertions including the active session WITH the env live, and the count stayed at 0 — demonstrating the scrub actually fires on new captures, not merely that an old leak was one-time-cleaned. (2) Bare 'hc-grc' preserved at 1446 files (the '~1400' project token), confirming no over-redaction of legitimate paths/repo refs. (3) The pre-commit hook exports ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS (default 'hc-grc', honoring an external value via ${VAR:-default}) on line 15 BEFORE invoking capture_transcripts.py on line 16. (4) Scrubber logic is correct: machine hostnames (auto-derived via socket/platform/scutil) redact bare+.local; ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS hosts redact ONLY .local. Both named tests pass and discriminate: test_precommit_hook parses split('capture_transcripts.py')[0] so it fails if the export moves after capture (verified via simulation); test_scrub forces a deterministic machine hostname and asserts machine bare+.local redaction, namespace .local-only redaction, bare-token preservation, and that unrelated settings.local.json is untouched (guards against a blanket .local rule). The vector is durable because the hook sets the env on every commit and the capture path reads it. The operator-hostname PII vector is now actually closed and durable against future captures.
concerns: Non-blocking observation (not a defect in this fix): _HOSTNAME_RES is computed at module import (capture_transcripts.py:251) from the then-current env, so the hook's shell-level export-before-process is load-bearing. Any future caller that invokes the scrubber without exporting ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS would silently skip the namespace .local rule (the machine-hostname rule is unconditional/auto-derived and remains robust). The committed pre-commit hook is the only capture entrypoint and sets it correctly, so the closure holds for the actual workflow.

content.text:
I've completed the adversarial fix-confirmation review of P9-PII-1 (round 2). Verdict: **CONFIRMED**.

The operator-hostname PII vector is now actually closed and durable. All four required verification points checked out, and most importantly the HEAD commit re-captured the active session with the env live while the leak count stayed at 0 — proving the scrub fires on new captures rather than just having cleaned an old leak once.
